_comment: Domain-classified catalog of agent skills. Role Router does NOT vendor skill bodies — each entry points to its original source and an install command (no redistribution risk). Roles map to the router: architect=/plan, builder=/build, worker=/review+/docs, meta=cross-cutting. Verify name resolution on first install; for aggregator repos fall back to the repo-level install. Re-check sources/licenses before relying on them.

install_mechanism.plugin: claude plugin marketplace add <owner/repo> && claude plugin install <plugin@marketplace>
install_mechanism.note: -g installs globally into ~/.claude/skills. Aggregator repos (mattpocock, alirezarezvani, openclaw) nest skills in category folders; if @skill 404s, run the repo-level install.

skills.name: react-native-skills
skills.description: React Native & Expo performance/best-practice rules (lists, animations, native modules).

skills.name: In-App Purchases
skills.description: IAP & subscriptions across iOS/Android/Flutter with RevenueCat, paywalls, receipt validation.

skills.name: flutter-coding-rules
skills.description: Dart/Flutter coding standards (clean architecture, widgets, state, minimal safe changes).

skills.name: flutter-performance
skills.description: Rebuild & memory optimization (const, buildWhen, ListView.builder, RepaintBoundary, Isolate).

skills.name: flutter-security
skills.description: OWASP Mobile security for Flutter (secure storage, pinning, obfuscation, jailbreak, PII).

skills.name: flutter-cicd
skills.description: CI/CD standards for Flutter (pipelines, fastlane, automated test/build/deploy).

skills.name: ui-ux-pro-max
skills.install: claude plugin marketplace add nextlevelbuilder/ui-ux-pro-max-skill && claude plugin install ui-ux-pro-max@ui-ux-pro-max-skill
skills.description: UI/UX design intelligence: 50+ styles, palettes, font pairings, UX guidelines across 10 stacks.

skills.name: supabase
skills.description: Supabase across Database/Auth/Edge Functions/Realtime/Storage; client + SSR integrations; RLS.

skills.name: supabase-postgres-best-practices
skills.description: Postgres performance & best practices: indexing, RLS performance, pooling, query tuning.

skills.name: senior-backend
skills.description: Senior backend engineering practices (API design, services, reliability).

skills.name: grill-with-docs
skills.description: Grill a plan against the domain model; sharpen terms; update CONTEXT.md + ADRs inline.

skills.name: grill-me
skills.description: Relentless interview of a plan/design until shared understanding across the decision tree.

skills.name: improve-codebase-architecture
skills.description: Find deepening/refactor opportunities informed by CONTEXT.md language and ADR decisions.

skills.name: prototype
skills.description: Throwaway prototype to flesh out a design (runnable logic app or UI variations).

skills.name: to-prd
skills.description: Turn conversation context into a PRD and publish it to the issue tracker.

skills.name: zoom-out
skills.description: Step back for broader context / higher-level perspective.

skills.name: to-issues
skills.description: Break a plan/PRD into independently-grabbable tracer-bullet issues on the tracker.

skills.name: triage
skills.description: Triage issues through a role-driven state machine (labels, AFK-agent prep).

skills.name: handoff
skills.description: Compact the current conversation into a handoff doc for another agent.

skills.name: setup-matt-pocock-skills
skills.description: Scaffold per-repo config (issue tracker, triage labels, CONTEXT/docs) the eng skills assume.

skills.name: tdd
skills.description: Red-green-refactor TDD loop; integration-test-first development.

skills.name: diagnose
skills.description: Disciplined bug/perf diagnosis: reproduce, minimise, hypothesise, instrument, regression-test.

skills.name: write-a-skill
skills.description: Author new agent skills with proper structure and progressive disclosure.

skills.name: find-skills
skills.description: Discover/install skills from the skills.sh registry by need.

skills.name: caveman
skills.description: Ultra-compressed output mode (~75% fewer tokens) — pairs well with cheap Worker Engines.
